title: Improve agent-config catalog and fix scoped-fragment projection
test_cli.py mutates real repo generated-manifest.json instead of using isolated copies
description: run_basicly() ran build/skills-build/check against the real REPO_ROOT for 6 tests instead of an isolated tmp copy, rewriting .basicly/generated-manifest.json's timestamp on every test run and dirtying the working tree after every pytest invocation (including pre-push). Fixed by adding a work_repo tmp_path fixture and routing all CLI invocations through an isolated copy, consolidating the 3 tests that already did this manually via local copytree calls.
close_reason: Fixed via work_repo tmp_path fixture in tests/test_cli.py (727af06).

title: Pin beads issue prefix in tracked config and ignore transient br artifacts
close_reason: pin prefix in tracked config, ignore transient br artifacts, document no-slug id rule

title: Rewrite core AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/copilot-instructions.md fragments for genericity and signal
description: Researched external agent-instruction repos (lattice, gastownhall/beads, JuliusBrussee skills, mattpocock/skills, ponytail) and redesigned the .basicly/core/fragments catalog: moved repo-specific content (project-defaults, python-style) into .basicly-local overlay, rewrote weak fragments (core-rules, quality-gate, if-stuck->decision-protocol, no-user-path-leakage->secure-coding) with concrete, high-signal guidance, added new generic fragments (knowledge-priming, session-completion, non-interactive-shell). Also fixed a real bug: applyTo in generated copilot instructions must be a string not a YAML array. Removed redundant source-fragments comment from generated file headers (context poisoning with no tooling use).
close_reason: Fragment catalog redesigned and committed in 0220a35: core fragments genericized, weak content rewritten, applyTo bug fixed.

title: fix ruff I001 import-sort violation masked by stale local .ruff_cache
description: GH Actions quality-gates failed ruff check on tests/test_loader.py and tests/test_skills.py (I001: missing blank line between third-party and first-party import groups). Every local ruff check this session reported clean because a stale .ruff_cache (gitignored, not present in CI's fresh checkout) was serving a false-clean cached result for these two files. Fixed the actual import formatting with ruff --fix after clearing the cache; verified clean with cache cleared.

title: harden commit-msg description rules and add self-improvement retro fragment
description: Two fixes from this session's real mistakes: (1) commit-msg.py and the conventional-commits skill said description must 'start lowercase' but the actual rule requires the whole description to be lowercase with no underscores -- both real rejections I hit. Clarified wording + added concrete invalid examples in both places. (2) Added project/self-improvement-retro fragment: at session end, if a real rejection/mistake occurred, propose the exact fragment/skill/hook wording fix that would have prevented it, present for explicit approval, never self-apply. Also deduped two now-redundant bullets (core-rules secrets/validation line duplicated by the broadened secure-coding fragment) to stay under the copilot 6000-char budget.

title: fold beads close into the resolving commit instead of a trailing chore commit
description: tool-br skill said to close issues 'when finished' without specifying commit sequencing, so I was closing after committing the fix, producing a separate near-empty chore: close <id> commit every time (e.g. 08e5e6a had zero code value). Updated Safe Defaults/Common Pitfalls to close before the resolving commit and fold .beads/issues.jsonl into it; amend if forgotten and unpushed, accept a trailing commit only if already pushed.
close_reason: tool-br skill updated to close-before-commit sequencing; this very commit demonstrates it.

title: Adopt br as the project task tracker
description: Add tool-br skill and enforce beads issue id in commit messages via a standalone git hook.
close_reason: tool-br skill and beads-commit-msg hook added and committed in 0220a35.

title: Support Conventional Commits breaking-change marker and add conventional-commits skill
description: commit-msg.py rejected the spec's '!' breaking-change indicator (feat!:, feat(scope)!:) with no way to express it. Added optional '!' support before the colon, shifted description group index, added regression tests. Also added .basicly/core/skills/conventional-commits/SKILL.md (with skill-creator-conformant Inputs/Outputs sections and trigger-quality validation) since no existing skill taught the full commit message format proactively before the hooks reject it.
close_reason: Breaking-change '!' support, conventional-commits skill, and clarified lowercase-description wording all committed (404adab, 25f4e7b).
